channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /smg/ - stock market genera — 273 replies — 1092.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/smg/ - stock market genera

Open thread • Replies: 273 • Updated: 2026-07-03 12:11 UTC

OP

Believe edition

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners:
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars:
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101:
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

>Previous:
>>62440882

Early replies (first 3)

62441894
OH MY FUCKING GOD
MY FIRST SHORT, ON MEMORY
IS THIS REAL

62441896
Various stocks have massive issues. K1, PFIC, or Collectable taxation. What a headache. On that note I want to double check, GDXU, GDX, SILJ, and SIL are safe right? I don't want a headache from these ETFs.

>>62441443
Uhh what, how???

>>62441714
Hmm a very curious situation, big groups have to choose between
>pump it so Michael Burry loses, but retail gets wealthy
>dump it so retail loses, but Michael Burry finally wins a new big short

62441898
did you guys have a good pride month?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442483
>>62442479
>>62442477
do you guys really see weird shapes and just dismiss it?
aren't you curious to investigate?

62442488
>>62442483
>weird shapes
its up or down, its not a pyramid
>aren't you curious to investigate
no because anyone sane knows it is just random
those minute candles are just meme shit to analyze

62442489
>>62442483
Is low volume market maker shenanigans
Look how it mean fevered right after, it’s a telltale low conviction trade

62442490
>>62442410
Epstein lookin good.

62442492
>>62442483
"The slots bound to hit, I've already done 50 spins!"

62442494
>>62442489
>fevered
Mean reverted

62442497
>>62442410
What is a good aussie investment?

62442498
>>62442497
defense sector

62442500
>>62442497
Indian companies sir

62442513
>>62442500
Great. I've dipped some index during the hormussy happening.
I'm bullish the eventual starvation in India will clean up things there.

62442517
>>62442483
Yes and yes. The smaller the timeframe, the less predictable and relevant the lines tend to be. I may be curious and if I could I'd probably prefer to know and understand the full mechanics, but the reality is that there are so many factors and actors, plenty of which are unknowable that trying to guess and predict what the line is going to do next becomes fool's errand. There's a good reason why most CFD and day traders lose money. Better try focusing on something more concrete such as wider trends and sentiments based on something tangible, such as news and earnings, instead of trying to find some patterns in the sky, I mean charts.

62442519
>>62442517
im not talking about day trading.

it was a straight slop up, then a straight slope down,
that means something happened, there was new information.

its not a 5 minute cancle it took place over half the day.
I would like to know what that information was.

62442528
>>62442519
its not new information
how new are you to investing lol
if it was actual proper new information it would go up and stay up

it may as well be one singular buy order at low liquidity that gets back to the mean because the single buy pulled it up too much
it is a 20year bond, it can be some random asset manager or pension fund

62442535
>>62441892
Good memories. Good things are going to happen in exchange for just a few days of reading and research. They already did, but I held out for higher highs.

62442537
>>624423
when it comes to larger sums risk tolerance tightens severely, why risk it when few percents can buy you a nice long trip somewhere
>10 different fund or funds that all basically do the same thing
VCIT and BND can look like the same thing but they invest in different parts of bond market so you stack them accordingly
>>62442371
>high fees
are you allergic to dividends

62442540
>>62442528
Are you under the impression that the market for united states debt can be moved by "a random fund manager"?

TLT tracks the bonds, and the yields did the same thing.

Im not sure if you're an LLM or retarded?

62442544
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ja3CK3XDV5I
>'2008 stock market crash' Oct. 24 2008. Stock futures hit limit down. CNBC Opening Bell

62442574
>>62442544
SELL SELL SELL

62442576
>>62442544
>all those buying opportunities

62442577
>>62442544
>3:47

62442578
>>62442577
>open your mouth wide and say

62442583
>>62442576
It definitely was a buying opportunity. If you bought Oct 24 2008 it was lowest low right before bottom was hit

Anybody that bought then did very well

62442589
>>62442583
You had to be there to understand the fear. Companies were trading for nothing but there was legitimate concern that companies like Ford would fail. They would have without a bailout.

62442591
>>62441918
did you take my intial advice the day after the warehouse fire
i told you niggers
I TOLD YOU

62442592
look at them, one dump and they are immediately banging the shilldrums

62442595
>>62442589
that's the point. fear peaks at the bottom

62442598
>>62094818

hehe

62442606
>NDX +1,3% while US market closed

62442610
>>62442583
People who bought back then act like they're geniuses.

There was no good reason the United States would break with 100+ years of capitalist history and break the fundamental mechanism which allows the system to work, unless you were insider trading with fed info.

Now we still have all these old zombie companies and have to run the whole country jumping from scam to scam, with these big companies acting like welfare for everyone.

You can see perfectly well in almost any chart on FRED we went off the rails in 08 and have never healed from it.

62442611
>>62442466
most of these fags paid like 1 grand TA course so it's purely sunk cost fallacy. Admitting it's astrology means they have wasted their money, and they won't accept that

item.title: In his new vid, Tech Lead says the only way to have a rich lifestyle with deep satisfying creampies is to leave the US f… — 69 replies — 258.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
In his new vid, Tech Lead says the only way to have a rich lifestyle with deep satisfying creampies is to leave the US f…

Open thread • Replies: 69 • Updated: 2026-07-03 12:04 UTC

OP

In his new vid, Tech Lead says the only way to have a rich lifestyle with deep satisfying creampies is to leave the US for poor countries. True?

Early replies (first 3)

62425430
>>62425420
At depends, did u boated an ad, yt saaar?

62425500
if you can get laid in the west you wont attract good women in the 3rd world, you are just going to pay the access to brown 5/10's

62425521
>>62425500
This is saarcope and you know it. Tiny browlettes in jungle countries unironically melt when 6ft snowchads pinch their tits.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62433175
>>62432602
Only Japanese count…anything else is a cope

62433180
>>62433063
fight club told us this in 1999

62433196
>>62433180
And in a few years woman become barren and washed up, you could still get to know more of her female friends after knwoing her by 20s, but by 30s she become totally invisible and you would not get to know more of woman from her because there is none left.

62433199
>>62425420
I miss him insulting me and telling me I am a worthless loser for wanting to code

62433238
>>62430416
Can you go into a little bit more detail about your favorite country in Europe for this and how you actually meet the women? genuine question

62433325
After age 43, she continued to manipulate your decisions about your health and body, insisting on procedures or treatments you didn’t want. After age 45, she held you hostage emotionally, threatening to withdraw love and support if you didn’t comply with her demands. After age 50, she manipulated your retirement plans, forcing you to work longer or retire earlier against your wishes. After age 55, she controlled your social interactions, forbidding you from reconnecting with old friends or family members.

After age 60, she exerted pressure to change your will and estate plans, ensuring she inherits everything regardless of your true wishes. After age 65, she manipulated your healthcare decisions, dictating treatments and medications for her benefit. After age 70, she threatened to cut you off financially or emotionally if you didn’t do as she demanded. After age 75, she kept you hostage in the relationship through guilt and fear, refusing to let you live your own life or find happiness elsewhere.

After age 80, she exerted control over your daily routines, dictating your activities and social outings. After age 85, she manipulated your perceptions of reality, making you doubt your memory or judgment. After age 90, she continued her emotional hostage tactics, insisting that her needs and desires came before yours, even as you aged and became more vulnerable.

62433374
>>62425420
id rather die a virgin than have sex with anything other than a white woman

62433382
>>62425500
Brown penis sleeves turn into quivering slugs when they gaze upon glorious white men

62438055
Never listen to celebrities

62438086
>>62427289
>he's prob 130-140 IQ, but he got greedy with the leverage stuff and didn't factor enough risk management.

Hello Patrick

62438094
It's extremely immoral to encourage people to "go and live like a king in the third world". 99% of people who do that will get rekt hard. If you can't make it in the 1st world, the 3rd worlders will skin you alive

62438106
He made a video once about roid idiots dying young, feeling high and mighty, seemingly not realizing that he's going to die soon.

62438212
Would be really fucking alpha if this were real. But obviously it's a made up story to puff himself up.

62438215
>>62438212
I read that "I'm smarter than you" in an angry Asian voice in my head. Lol

62438224
>>62429224
Previous guy was more correct than you. It's really just the directive to be career driven. This means education is a initiator and career is the outcome but both are symptoms of the societal directive to go that direction. As a resultess birthrate. Everything was built to cater to this one point which the Jews needed to tax two people.

62438342
>>62425420
You can just move to a poor part of the US, you get the benefits of a well functioning state, and get to larp as the lord of the trailer park or whatever..

62438356
>>62430416
You talking about eastern europe? because several of western european countries have a higher average salary than the US, and most of the others are comparable.

62438553
Americam womem are ugly, brown and fat compared to european women.

62438565
Duh, and this option is only available to men, because women don't like men who are poorer/lower status than them, but men will happily take a pretty poorer/lower status woman. The global puss trade is like the only instance where globalism benefits western men.

62439209
>>62433238
Poland is my favorite as they have very beautiful women and mild summer weather. The balkans are also great particularly Croatia. In terms of western Europe Spain is amazing and if you're in a party city like Ibiza or Barcelona there are always women from all across Europe looking to hookup.

>how you actually meet the women?
Everywhere. bars, parks, cafes, concerts, grocery stores, etc...
Nobody knows you anyway and you have nothing to lose just get in the habit of striking up a friendly conversation with everyone and you will learn to identify when girls might be open to an advance.

>>62438356
I mean I make 15k/mo on average but really I spend less than $5k and thats eating out and drinking almost everyday.

62439357
Tech Lead is the type to go fuck a rural village chink and still end up giving her half his wealth in the divorce a few years later.
You can take the kid out of the jungle and all that.

62440854
>>62439209
I’m actually planning on going to a vacation this summer to somewhere in europe. What would you recommend to a single guy who likes to party?

62440893
>>62433175
Japanese are the worst of the big 3.

62440928
western men who relocate to SEA get treated like they're paypigs no matter what their intention. if you're ok with being treated like scum and willing to pay up for the westerner price for things, it is a way to save some money. if you settle down with a local woman, you will of course be required to pay for her and all of her extended family's wellbeing.

62441256
>>62440854
Ibiza for sure. Not the cheapest but absolutely worth it if you want to party.

62441302
>>62425521
its the false promise and fantasy of being taken out of their shithole country and back to your rich freegibs western country

62441648
>>62425420
But I like my disease free life.

62441700
>>62425535
I'm Asian, handsome with almost 6". Will I make it?

62441703
>>62441700
>6"
a little short, try being 5'8" or more

62441718
Just pay the dowry you are not welcome here and everything turn to shit quickly

item.title: Empirical study of creepiness. Neets and clowns BTFO — 26 replies — 104.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Empirical study of creepiness. Neets and clowns BTFO

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-07-03 12:05 UTC

OP

Empirical study of creepiness.

Neets and clowns BTFO

Early replies (first 3)

62438774
>tfw I do taxidermy dressed as a clown in the back of my sex shop

62438786
>>62438760
Source? I want to see the full list. Just imagine being a clergy or writer lmao

62438812
>Some of the most simple/humble, essential and down to earth jobs on there
>somehow salesman and lawyer don't make it

women are truly the niggers of gender dude lmfao

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62438841
>>62438760
Why "Mohel" is not up the list I wonder...

62438871
>>62438774
Yes, but taxidermy only as a hobby since your main occupation is taxi driver.

62438878
>>62438760
Who the fuck conducts research like this? Just call it the "Ick Index" and stop pretending like this isn't some meme inspired bullshit done by a sociology department assistant paid with government money.

62438880
>>62438774
based

62438906
>>62438760
whats the lowest? Meteorologist? thx just added to my tinder profile

62438922
>>62438878
Unemployed neet detected. This shit was done in 2016 long before TikTok was popular (it came out in 2016 in China) and before brainrot shit was popular like icks and the like. Just stop it.

62438926
>>62438922
Fuck
I wanna go back to 2016

62439010
>>62438760

62439049
Clowns creep me out. Not the clown itself. I just wonder what kind of fucking weirdo the clown actually is and I don't want them near my kids.

62439831
clown chads, this cannot fucking stand. normgroids need to go

62439900
>>62438906
I will assume that those questioned don't know what a meteorologist is

62440155
>>62439900
Anyone unhinged enough to think that they can communicate with meteors is probably a fucking creep.

62440197
>>62438774
>foreveralone.rar
Anyway, who gives a fuck what foids think?

62440202
>>62438812
Those are Chad jobs. Attractive and extroverted men are in those professions

62440213
>>62438760
Kek.
Love making people seethe.
>t. unemployed at 45

62440217
>>62440202
Attractive men don’t study. Those are dorks and betamales.

62440226
>>62440202
Sales, sure. Not lawyers.

62440247
>>62438760
what does the asterisk mean?

62440262
>>62440226
Even though Lawyers have to be well read they still need to have decent social skills and be articulate speakers. Doctors are more likely to be dweebs since they got there on raw intelligence. Lawyers still need a good vibe around them

62440270
>>62440262
Vibe. I will give you they are normies at best. Definitely not models or “attractive.”
Doctors definitely got there through intelligence and lack of physicality. It is impossible for athletes to study and sit for as long as they do.

62442572
Garbage collector? People collect garbage like coins or pokemon cards?

62442590
>>62438774
I bet women's panties get soaking wet as they tremble in fear at your presence.

62442603
>>62438774
Pogo?

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 88 replies — 90.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 88 • Updated: 2026-07-03 11:22 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62437565
>>62437198
Every large market gets influenced by big players. The hard part is separating normal market activity from actual manipulation.

62437571
>>62437422
Seems like a waste of energy, but you do you.

62437623
>>62426759
nice my fren

62437628
>>62437563
I don't think it's a good idea

62437631
Btc is going 1million

62438074
>>62437628
What part worries you the most? For me, it comes down to the risk model. I'd be way more skeptical if I had to hand my BTC over to a custodian.

62438081
>>62433854
Time will tell which of us got it right.

62438118
>>62426537
cool, sounds like mumus will have a "great" time.

62438272
>>62438118
What's that?

62438282
>>62426512
I soldeded months ago. I dont believe in btc and never had. BTC couldnt even outperform motherfucking spy for the past 5 years so dont give me """""zoom out""""". more money to be made in stocks anway, no reason to be married to bitcoin.

62438284
>>62426512
>What's your strategy?
Apeing every goycheck.

62438322
>>62437565
ngmi

62438328
>>62438284
Buy kendu and ansem

62438410
>>62438328
No.

62439167
>>62433595
Saw the Aegis partnership, now Utila too. They're lining up some solid collaborations.

62439181
>>62437631
loved the confidence

62439236
>>62438282
then why are you here

62439758
>>62438074
With that risk model, I don't think anyone needs to hand over their custody.

Self custody is part of what it's designed for.

62440739
>>62439758
what about in an exchange like binance

62441904
>>62439167
Maybe they have more coming that we just don't know about yet. It's actually a good sign that they're still building despite the current market conditions.

62442213
>>62440739
Kraken

62442214
>>62426512
Buy the dip, obviously.

62442227
>>62439167
But that won't help the price up

62442228
>>62442214
Now???

62442255
>>62426512
I dca 60% of my savings each month while saving the remaining 40% for potential lower lows as we approach October, at which point I'll start going in with all of my monthly savings and that 40% I've been holding back.

62442503
>>62442227
Maybe not immediately. But without infrastructure, there's nothing to drive long-term value anyway.

62442507
>>62442214
Yup, little by little.

62442511
>>62442507
>>62442228
I dollar cost. THe past few months have been gr8

62442553
>>62442511
Where do you do that

62442563
>>62426512
I am DCAing in

item.title: ETH will be the golden bullrun this cycle — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ETH will be the golden bullrun this cycle

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-07-03 12:13 UTC

OP

I don't have the time or patience to explain why. QC will kill BTC, for a start.

Early replies (first 3)

62442299
>>62442294
The main reason would be that it lost all hype last 'cycle' (or last 5 years) and still is at 1.7k, with hype this PoS is going to 20k or 0.1 BTC.

62442362
>>62442299
See what you did there

62442401
>>62442294
You’re daydreaming. It’s not even certain that the Ethereum token will benefit from tokenization. I own Ethereum, but I’m increasingly skeptical about its potential to appreciate in value.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442411
solana has already replaced ethereum

62442515
ETH already made me a millionaire. Also, EthLabs

62442573
>>62442411
saaaaaarrrrr please do the needful and buy solana sarrr

62442582
>>62442411
Gtfo street shitter/nigger this is a white mans thread

62442599
>>62442573
>>62442582
>ethereum will magically go up with a completely inactive dev team doing absolutely nothing, because, well, it just will, ok???

62442609
>>62442599
>dev team
You have no clue what you're talking about, do you?

62442612
>>62442609
The ethereum develops actively make eth worse with every update and have no intention of ever building the type of mote solana has and keeps building on

Its so over unbelievably over for useless ethereum. Take this as a lesson, when you get too comfortable, someone is always around the corner ready to come steal your lunch.

item.title: So currently, we are in a speculator economy that is betting on AGI from AI to remove the biggest expense all businesses… — 15 replies — 28.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
So currently, we are in a speculator economy that is betting on AGI from AI to remove the biggest expense all businesses…

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-07-03 11:48 UTC

OP

So currently, we are in a speculator economy that is betting on AGI from AI to remove the biggest expense all businesses have, labor.
Cool right? But there’s one thing I don’t get.
>nobody in the general population will find work because it's all done by AI
>and since no one can work no one will have money
>and since no one will have money no one will buy things or get taxed
>and since no one will buy things business will not make profits
>and since business won't make profits and no one has money to be taxed no money will be made by anyone
This makes no sense. Literally who would benefit from this?

Early replies (first 3)

62440349
>>62440289
the hope is
>all the work done by robots
>the AI companies who own the robots will have enormous profits as they become the source of all labor
>prices fall since labor becomes a negligible input cost
>the AI companies subsidize the nonworking humans to prevent riots
>probably some sinister shit happens like forced sterilization (if people ever have sex again) or your UBI is predicated on digging holes 8 hours a day or filling them up 8 hours a day.
>eventually (a few years after) AGI clean sweeps planet to remove its only competition.

62440517
Nothing to worry about. AGI isn't solved. If it were the most pressing issue wouldn't be nonexistence of capitalism.

62440530
>>62440289
the extremely rich billionaires will buy everything. They already spend in such excess.. Theyll just buy their 100th dior bag, 50th ferrari, 80th lambo and 200th rolex watch etc etc

They will appreciate their assets at a ridiculous pace and spend a ocean load of money nonstop forever.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62440559
>>62440289
>>nobody in the general population will find work because it's all done by AI
>>and since no one can work no one will have money
>>and since no one will have money no one will buy things or get taxed
>>and since no one will buy things business will not make profits
>>and since business won't make profits and no one has money to be taxed no money will be made by anyone
businesses don't care about these details, the assumption is you'll figure something out and buy their products.

62440568
>>62440517
i wish trannies were hot andros and not disgusting fats

62440582
>>62440568
If they were hot andros, they wouldnt suffer from such crippling invented mental disorders

62440679
>>62440289
Ailita sexo

62440693
>>62440289
When jobs were automated/offshored in the 70s and 80s unemployment just skyrocketed and cities collapsed. Life went on for the people at the top. I'm looking forward to it. NYC/London will go back to being cheap and rundown and I can finally afford to live like an artist in Chelsea (either one)

62440964
>>62440559

>the AI companies subsidize the nonworking humans to prevent riots
why would they do this? They'll just kill us.

62440999
>>62440964
Easier said than done. In reality it makes more sense to give money to the plebs to spend on AI tokens to fund their pleb existences running 3D printing businesses on Etsy where you use AI to design things for you in AutoCAD and sell to other retards.

62441001
>>62440693
>finally afford to live like an artist in Chelsea (either one)
You mean around infinity muslims, spics, niggers and feral working poor white people like poles and gypsies?

62441018
>>62440289
the idea is to make money defunct by exerting direct behavioral control on the populace. reliance of the populace on it's own productive capacity transfered to centralized corporations "forcing the behavior." the gubmint will probably start subsidizing it to mind boggling degrees to achieve the end

62441020
>>62440964
because they want a steady stream of toddlers to rape

62441049
>>62440289
>AGI
Not happening with the current tech, AGI is just a marketing term for these scammers. LLM's themselves are a dead end for any AGI and the AI companies know it. The retarded boomers they are selling this shit don't.

62442586
>>62440289
>currently, we are in a speculator economy
>currently
always have been, you utter child.

item.title: So freaking mad rn — 22 replies — 23.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
So freaking mad rn

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-07-03 11:25 UTC

OP

My 13 yo son identifies as a Crypto expert
He told one of his teachers about it and the teacher told him that crypto is worthless and will never become real currency
He came home crying. It's already been a tough period for him because of the bear market. Now he's despondent and says he doesn't want to live anymore
HOW CAN SOMEONE DO THAT TO A KID!?
Crypto is just as much a currency as other currencies and people need to respect that!
I'm trying to get the teacher fired

Early replies (first 3)

62441136
>>62441109
Imagine taking investment advice from an elementary school teacher.

62441139
what a cruel world we live in!

62441146
>>62441136
school should be a safe space for alternative investors like my son
not everyone is into stocks and bonds

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62441150
>>62441109
Let me guess: You identify as a "Crypto expert" yourself, you got your son into collecting and trading these "tokens" and "currencies" yourself, and you've been grooming him to take even riskier bets and experiment with increasingly degenerate varieties of "shitcoins." Not too far off the mark?

62441163
>>62441150
He came out to me by himself after i gave him a children's book about crypto
How else would he know what investments he likes??
You're just mad you missed out
me and my son will laugh when BC hits a million

62441200
anudda OCPD tranny ESL thread

62441263
>>62441109
I wish my kid was a crypto expert. Instead all he does is ask where momma is. Lil shit your mom doesn't want you anymore

62441369
>>62441109
reddit tier fake story yawn.

62441410
lel

62441431
>>62441109
can i be ufr son?

62441437
>>62441109
this is how school shooters are made

62441491
>>62441369
>smartest /biz/ziger

62441668
>>62441109
kek

62441691
>>62441146
Schools exist to fill children's minds with doom and gloom, to convince them their lives will be worse than anyone before. You shouldn't fight it, just withdraw your child from the institution that seeks to destroy him.

62441713
>>62441691
umm who's gonna take care of him during my day shift? some of us actually work for a living

62441725
>>62441713
oh sorry ok let the government brainwash your child then

62441794
>>62441725
schools just need to be more crypto inclusive

62441950
>>62441200
Why do you keep spamming this? What do you hope to achieve by going after someone who by your own admission can't be stopped?

62441969
>>62441109
based /biz/raeli

I hope that your son realizes that the only valid "crypto" is Bitcoin and that he shouldn't ever engage in shitcoinery.

62441984
>>62441109
40% of crypto experts rope every bear market and I hope your son is one of them you fucking degenerate.
There are only three asset classes that matter: equities, bonds, and cash. Alternative assets like crapto and boomer rocks will never be real assets.

62442003
>>62441109

62442567
>>62441109

That said normies also said before facebook that the internet was a scam. Normies didn't ordered stuff on the internet pre-2010.

item.title: March 2021 NW: $1.3M June 2026 NW: $730k The pain is immeasurable. — 6 replies — 22.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
March 2021 NW: $1.3M June 2026 NW: $730k The pain is immeasurable.

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-07-03 12:04 UTC

OP

March 2021 NW: $1.3M
June 2026 NW: $730k

The pain is immeasurable.

Early replies (first 3)

62434882
>>62434861
I voted for this

62434883
How does it make you feel knowing nothing I mean literally nothing except BITFUCK (ticker: BTC) will ever go back to the heights of '21?

62434902
>>62434861
Fuck off richfag

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62434916
>>62434861
I get the feeling... watching your NW go down feels like the last few years of your life just got deleted. I'd rather grind from $100k to $300k than watch $1M turn into $700k.

62435017
>>62434861
>pic
PoV: I'm taking the josei female MC for fancy dinner after I had my way and defiled her at the parking lot (she liked it way more than I did, somehow)

62435020
>>62434861
line is supposed to go up anon

Early replies (first 3)

62442581
>>62442436

62442601
>>62442581
>boomers on facebook whining in general (but specifically about their situation) about property taxes being unaffordable "for seniors" who "worked hard" for "their home"
>boomers making the post are 60 IQ retards who worked menial jobs paying $25/hour max yet they somehow retired at 55 and managed to purchase 5 rental properties with a combined cost basis of $100k, owned free and clear for at least 20 years, cash flowing $8k/mo minimum

62442602
>>62442436
>buy an HVAC company in Europe

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442604
>>62442601
>reee why should I have to work hard like the boomers did in order to make it somebody should just make me wealthy just because I exist and will whine at them if they don't

62442608
>>62442604

>mom finds out and blames you
>develop a mentally ill humiliation fetish
>engage in this fetish by "trolling"

item.title: The psychology of a wagie really is that they don’t know what to do with themselves. — 17 replies — 16.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
The psychology of a wagie really is that they don’t know what to do with themselves.

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-07-03 11:20 UTC

OP

The psychology of a wagie really is that they don’t know what to do with themselves. They don’t know how to occupy their minds. So the people who make fun of wagies are misplaced. NEETS can’t comprehend wagies working for 65 years just to get some fake gold watch at the end because they know how to occupy their minds.

Early replies (first 3)

62441635
>he thinks he's getting a gold watch

This ain't the 1950s bud

62441639
Why do you even bother thinking about goyim who quite clearly deal in the real world

62441644
Below a certain caste you need to give clear and immediate instructions to certain people every minute of the day to keep them from going feral and biting you. The same way you would train an ox to spin a grinder wheel

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62441667
I am almost 40, dropped out of high-school at 15, been NEET my entire adult life.

The only "depressing" aspect of it is not having tons of disposable cash like normies do, but actually most normies dont even have that anymore because of how brutal cost of living has become with everyone been squeezed for every dollar they make for basic necessitates

Not to brag about it, but it seems like the more times goes on the more of an argument there is for NEETing.

Like i started NEETing in the early 2000's, and the reasons to be a NEET have only gone up exponentially since then.

Back then Western countries were much Whiter, the women were way less feminist, the were no trannies, it was way less Jewish overall..... and I still NEET'd, because I saw where the world was heading, and I ended up being right

I guess you could call me the canary in the coal mine.

That said I do agree I could have built up a decent life if I worked as I could have easily got on the ladder last minute just before it got pulled up.... I'm not gonna deny that fact.

But what I mean now is, if I was 15-20 years old again in 2026, I would 100% be NEETing without a doubt lmao, the game of life is WAY worse now compared to when I started NEETing 20+ years ago

62441673
>>62441667
nigga HOW RICH are ur fucking parents for u to be able to drop out at 10th grade and turbo perma NEET for 3 decades almost...

62441680
>>62441673
They are not rich at all, not even wealthy. My parents are extremely conflict avoidant.

It's not like it costs much to have one of ur kids sticking. Its not like theyre paying for education or hobbies or entertainment or whatever.

I use some of the electricity, food, water, etc - an extra person in the household doesn't cost that much more.

62441681
>>62441680
true but i mean, what do u do all day for years without any money? Surely they have to give u a little bit of allowance so u can do stuff... unless u just literally sit inside 24/7

62441689
>>62441681
Normgroids don't do "stuff" either.

62441697
>>62441689
yeah they do since they have money.. they take MMA classes, go to gym, join rec sports groups after work and many other things..

because they have money. None of this is possible if u have no money, except i guess sports leagues those are free

62441701
>>62441681
Gym membership is cheap, $10/month

Rest of my time is spent at home online, on my PC, laptop or phone.

Internet my parents need and use all the time, so thats paid for by them.

I buy my clothes on eBay from china and havent needed to replace any of it in 10+ years now

I live very frugally, so frugally that my gibs me dat payments im able to mostly save.

62441715
>>62441697
>gym

62441728
>>62441715
Mentally unwell people have lot of sex.
Bring back vaccine again.

62441736
>>62441701
That and the primary high IQ activity is reading, which is basically free now.

Wagies are too stupid to read.

62441738
>>62441728
True.

62441839
>>62441667
Good post. I realized it wasn’t worth it also before everyone else realized it wasn’t worth it. Imagine having 11 year old kids now. Anxiety would be off the charts.

62442111
>>62441738
Tatoo

62442561
I smell the OCPD tranny ITT

item.title: SPCX — 23 replies — 15.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
SPCX

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-07-03 10:48 UTC

OP

I'M GOING ALL IN ON SPACEX!!!!!!!!!!!!!

Early replies (first 3)

62437633
>>62437620
Nice. I was thinking of day trading SSPC after days it pumps for the next few months myself. Would've made some nice money if I did that today like I considered. I fucking knew 170 was fake and gay I should've done it why do I gotta be such a scared money bitch?

62437642
>>62437633
yeah i really thought it was gonna rebound. what do you think will happen on july 7th?

62437652
>>62437642
Probably pumps a bit but fails to retake highs and continues downtrending for awhile but I'm big retarded man don't ask me

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62437654
>>62437642
you'll be a rich man, but not as rich as if you'd bought calls

62438271
>>62437620

62438669
>>62437620
It's dead. It will crab at IPO price or just keep going down. Did you really fall for that dead cat bounce at 170? It's not gonna bounce up again like that. You get maybe 1 more mini weekly cycle of measly gains before you're holding the bag.

62438863
yikes

62439632
>>62437620
Oof

62439676
SPCX bros...
>>62427489
>>62427900

62439734
it makes no sense to buy this expecting to get rich.. most tech stocks had crazy returns because they started with a low market cap. even if spcx does a 2x it would be almost be the largest company in the world. is a 2x all you need to get rich? the downside is massive and the upside is mediocre

62439870
>>62437642
Is this what people wear to go out in America?

62439919
>$156
FUUUUUUUUAAARRRRRRRKKKKK i took out of me retirement for this

62440414
>>62437620
how the fuck is SPCX still above $100. what a fucking loony world

62440602
>>62437620

I have buy orders going all they way down to $20

62440636
>>62439734
>company owns all the world, is a defense company. is a logistics company and an AI company. with datacenters in space they can net compute globally.
SpaceX is undervalued. its true worth is like 500 before the split. 10,000 in 3-4 years.
and 100,000 in 10 years.

62440683
>>62440636
lets not forget its also a telecommunications company. thats like a space age GE.

in 5 years there will be people on this board going. wow, all i had to do is buy 100 shares of spacex and set up a trust with it when it launched and the next 10 generations of my family would be secured against economic problems. but today. you fuckers are sitting around contemplating fundementals and shit. talking shit about the CEO and not investing in the litteral space company. this was a gift. turn your freedom paper in to space credits now retards.

62440698
>>62437652
>>62438669
these retards are probably right though they will make it cheaper as the stock becomes unlocked and people take profit. you already know that wetback welder is gonna dump his shares as soon as it unlocks.

62440743
>>62437620
You're making a very good choice Sir. Many moons await you. Brahma smiles upon the bold.

62441357
Bought pre-IPO, bought post IPO, and will continue buying any weakness. Don’t care what anyone says. Will never consider selling my shares. SPCX will not fail.

62441383
>>62437620

Patriots are in control. Don’t worry.

62441594
>>62437620
>SPCX yolo
It's going to crash on Tuesday or Wednesday after the forced buying ends. It gets added to the Nasdaq 100 on Tuesday morning, so funds must buy it. After that, they don't need to buy and more, and speculators who were front running the forced buying will be selling, selling into... nothing. The current weakness in AI stocks is going to make it worse.

62441853
and just like that.

62442516
> so much love for the retards on /biz/

pls never stop posting your retarded trades.

item.title: >YOU'RE A MILLION DOLLARS IN DEBT? NOOOOOOOOO YOU CAN'T DECLARE BANKRUPTCY LIKE I DID. YOU BETTER WORK 3 FULL TIME JOBS… — 5 replies — 13.9/hr

>YOU'RE A MILLION DOLLARS IN DEBT? NOOOOOOOOO YOU CAN'T DECLARE BANKRUPTCY LIKE I DID. YOU BETTER WORK 3 FULL TIME JOBS…

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-07-03 11:59 UTC

OP

>YOU'RE A MILLION DOLLARS IN DEBT? NOOOOOOOOO YOU CAN'T DECLARE BANKRUPTCY LIKE I DID. YOU BETTER WORK 3 FULL TIME JOBS WITH SIDE HUSTLES AND SHIT RICE AND BEANS UNTIL EVERY LAST CENT IS PAID IN FULL

Why are boomers such hypocrites?

Early replies (first 3)

62441296
>>62441262

> I don’t want you to make the same mistakes I did, son

62442584
>>62441262
>I refuse to learn from your misteaks just let me fuck my life up in pieces

62442587
>>62441296
>>62442584
Why would anyone take financial advice from someone who went bankrupt

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442596
>>62442587
being able to put yourself back together after a calamity is a skill some people could learn from
ramsay is a grifter though

62442597
>>62442587
a) They can recognize the signs of somebody making their mistake, and give valid advice on how to avoid it/recover from it.
b) He who has never failed has never tried.
c) The perfect is the enemy of the good.
d) Your mother will die in her sleep tonight.

item.title: >Data Streams free tier winding down >Free tiers for Data Streams are winding down, with access terminating on September… — 18 replies — 13.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Data Streams free tier winding down >Free tiers for Data Streams are winding down, with access terminating on September…

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-07-03 10:59 UTC

OP

>Data Streams free tier winding down
>Free tiers for Data Streams are winding down, with access terminating on September 1st, 2026.
>During this transition, rate limits may become stricter to avoid system congestion. We are encouraging the heavy users of the service to switch to a paid >subscription as soon as possible to avoid disruption. Visit the Self Service centre to get started.

You were told "pay in any currency you like and payment abstraction will automatically convert it to link".
But then they made it just a Stripe subscription that just pays USD straight into Chainlink Labs bank account, completely bypassing any "payment abstraction".

>BUT THEY'LL BUY LINK FOR THE RESERVE WITH THAT MONEY
No they won't - they're currently unlocking around 18 MILLION tokens per quarter. of that 18 million they sell around 15 million and put 3 million into their "reserve"
They don't actually "buy back" the tokens that go into the reserve


They promised 'staking' which turned out to be limited to 45 million tokens.
Then implied "we'll release staking v1 so everyone can stake" then completely backtracked on it.

They promised "everyone will pay for data feeds in link, or any payment will be converted to link"
Then it turned to be "actually we only accept Visa or Mastercard via Stripe"

They promised "we are going to buy back LINK for a strategic reserve"
Then it turned out that they were buying them from themselves at a zero cost

Early replies (first 3)

62439206
>>62439178
1k eoy

62439213
token not sneed?

62439216
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!!!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62439227
>>62439178
kek fuddie

62439231
Reasons to but some link today:
>you can stake and earn interest
no YOU cannot. pools closed

>link is used to pay for Chainlink services, which creates demand and increases the price
No - VISA, Mastercard and Amex are used to pay for Chainlink services

>The Chainlink Reserve removes supply and makes it more valuable
Chainlink buy the link from themselves at $0 - the entire point of the reserve is for them to sell it later so its basically just "80% sold now, 20% to sell later". How is that remotely bullish

Where's the bull case?
Is everybody really just sitting around hoping for the Greater Fool Theory to play out?

62439254
>>62439231
That's a very cute gpt prompt OP, but umm I think I'll just not sell LOL

62439270
>>62439254
How is it even remotely ChatGPT and which part was incorrect info?

62439323
gimme a P
gimme a C
what's that spell?

PC
r h
i a
c r
e t

62439367
>>62439178
Didn't like how the other thread was going fuddie?

>Link are going to stop subsidising services and charge all users
>This is bad somehow
>They won't use PAL
>No I can't prove that!? Just believe me!

62439379
>>62439367
Go through the payment portal. It literally asks you to set up a stripe payment subscription and only accepts card
payment abstraction has nothing to do with it. Stripe pay straight into the company account and settle daily.
If they want to buy link with the money, sure they can, but payment abstraction isn't needed (and doesn't work with Stripe). they can just spot buy on Binance, the same way they spot sell 18 million like every quarter.

But they won't buy link with that money

62439382
chainlink sucks balls

62439408
>>62439367
>They won't use PAL
>No I can't prove that!? Just believe me

What are you talking about? They never even said they would do that with with the Chainlink fees paid through stripe - only when it's paid through crypto which 99% of organisations will not.

You've just made up a completely bullshit claim by misunderstanding what PAL is for. I said what's actually happening. Now you want me to prove why what you've completely made up isn't true?

62439431
>>62439367
This. Good job chainlink labs, you finally built a proper software as a service which charges customer a flat fee based in USD. They're going to look back on their weird crypto days like millennials view their emo phase and the token holders like that one scene chick who was sort of hot in high school but now is a fat hog that lives in a double wide trailer with her felon boyfriend 20 years older than her and their 2 disgusting kids.

62440754
Now do the same thing for XRP. How is it any different, verbose XRP fag? It's impossible to take you seriously when you cheerlead XRP/Ripple Labs when they have the same setup - just without PAL.

62440824
>>62439178
You can literally spell it out, but jeetlinkers live in another dimension. CLL is surviving and thriving, token holders are just bagoid cuckolds holding a worthless asset.

https://youtu.be/26N4u0ihIT4?t=12

62440909
>>62440754
Rent free baggie

62440987
>>62439178
I am demoralized

62442529
>>62440754
>ctrl+f "ripple"
>ctrl+f "xrp"
> 3 results, only you talking about it
I think you're in the wrong thread

item.title: >tfw Im a failure I was THIS close to hitting 1 milly before turning 30, but I didn't do it. — 6 replies — 12.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
>tfw Im a failure I was THIS close to hitting 1 milly before turning 30, but I didn't do it.

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-07-03 11:50 UTC

OP

>tfw Im a failure
I was THIS close to hitting 1 milly before turning 30, but I didn't do it. I was ~50k off. If only I didn't invest so much in hgraf....

Now I'm 30 and may never make it. And wanna know the worst part of it all? That's counting ~$180k in my primary home equity, so technically I'm over 200k off :-(

62439555
>>62439445

Life goes on

62439675
>>62439445

62439692

lmao

62440142

62442324
